perturbation.original_text: The Company hereby agrees to engage the Consultant in accordance with the terms and provisions hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company may consider to engage the Consultant in accordance with the terms and provisions hereof.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity by replacing 'agrees to engage' with 'may consider to engage', making the engagement of the consultant discretionary rather than obligatory. This contradicts the overall agreement which assumes a defined engagement, creating uncertainty on the enforcement of consultant's rights. Specifically, it contradicts the section where duties and compensation are established assuming consultant will have responsibilites and be paid for.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Duties : The Consultant's services hereunder will be provided on the basis of the following terms and conditions: (a) The Consultant will report directly to the chief executive officer and/or other officers of the Company; (b) The Consultant will be responsible for advising on and facilitating the Company's investor relations, corporate structure and governance, public relations and commercial development activities and supervising, liaising and instructing outside service providers, in each case, subject to any applicable law and to instructions provided by the officers of the Company from time to time. 2 (c) The Consultant will faithfully, honestly and diligently serve the Company and cooperate with the Company and utilize maximum professional skill and care to ensure that all services rendered hereunder are to the satisfaction of the Company, acting reasonably, and the Consultant will provide any other services not specifically mentioned herein, but which by reason of the Consultant's capability, the Consultant knows or ought to know to be necessary to ensure that the best interests of the Company are maintained. (d) The Consultant will assume, obey, implement and execute such duties, directions, responsibilities, procedures, policies and lawful orders as may be determined or given from time to time by the Company. (e) The Consultant will report the results of his duties hereunder to the Company as it may request from time to time.
perturbation.justification: The original text created an obligation for the Company to engage the Consultant. The changed text makes the engagement discretionary ('may consider to engage'). This directly contradicts the 'Duties' section which assumes the Consultant 'will' perform specific duties and responsibilities. If the Company only 'may consider' engaging the consultant, then the duties outlined in the contradicted section become unenforceable and create an inconsistency within the document.

perturbation.original_text: All subsequent monthly payments of Base Consulting Fee shall be paid within the first five days of the following calendar month.
perturbation.changed_text: All subsequent monthly payments of Base Consulting Fee should generally be paid within the first five days of the following calendar month.
perturbation.explanation: The introduction of 'should generally' replaces a mandatory payment term with a discretionary one. This contradicts the premise of Article 2 establishing compensation, by creating uncertainty about the right of the consultant to receive payment on time, and weakens the compensation clause, and specifically introduces contradictions on the second part of 2.1 a) where increases to the Base Consulting Fee can be made and must be review annually.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remuneration. (a) The Consultant's monthly base consulting fee shall be three thousand two hundred fifty United States dollars ($3,250 (together with any increases thereto as hereinafter provided, the "Base Consulting Fee") through 19 August 2020. Commencing on 20 August 2020, Consultant's Base Consulting Fee shall be increased to $8,500 per calendar month. The Base Consulting Fee shall be payable in accordance with the Company's normal payroll procedures in effect from time to time. All subsequent monthly payments of Base Consulting Fee should generally be paid within the first five days of the following calendar month. The Base Consulting Fee may be increased by the Board from time to time during the Term, but shall be reviewed by the Board at least annually
perturbation.justification: The original text states that monthly payments 'shall be paid within the first five days', establishing a mandatory obligation. The changed text replaces 'shall' with 'should generally', making the payment timeline discretionary. This contradicts the 'Remuneration' section which outlines the 'Base Consulting Fee' and any 'increases thereto', implying a guaranteed and timely payment. The discretionary nature introduced by 'should generally' weakens the consultant's entitlement to timely payment, creating a conflict with the guaranteed remuneration structure described in the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: The Consultant agrees that, upon termination of his services for the Company, he will immediately surrender to the Company all Company Confidential Information then in his possession or under his control.
perturbation.changed_text: The Consultant agrees that, upon termination of his services for the Company, he will endeavor to surrender to the Company all Company Confidential Information then in his possession or under his control.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will immediately surrender' to 'will endeavor to surrender' weakens the obligation to return confidential information upon termination, it introduces ambiguity. This change creates a contradiction with Section 3.1 (b), which establishes strict confidentiality. It implies that the consultant is not fully bound to protect confidential information after termination, impacting the Company's proprietary rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Maintenance of Confidential Information. (a) The Consultant acknowledges that, in the course of performing his obligations hereunder, the Consultant will, either directly or indirectly, have access to and be entrusted with confidential information (whether oral, written or by inspection) relating to the Company or its respective affiliates, associates or customers (the "Confidential Information"). (b) The Consultant acknowledges that the Company's Confidential Information constitutes a proprietary right, which the Company is entitled to protect. Accordingly, the Consultant covenants and agrees that, as long as he works for the Company, the Consultant will keep in strict confidence the Company's Confidential Information and will not, without prior written consent of the Company, disclose, use or otherwise disseminate the Company's Confidential Information, directly or indirectly, to any third party. (c) The Consultant agrees that, upon termination of his services for the Company, he will endeavor to surrender to the Company all Company Confidential Information then in his possession or under his control.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated the Consultant 'will immediately surrender' confidential information, which creates a firm obligation. The changed text modifies this to 'will endeavor to surrender,' which weakens the obligation and introduces ambiguity regarding the Consultant's responsibility to return confidential information. This directly contradicts Section 3.1 (b), which states that 'the Company's Confidential Information constitutes a proprietary right, which the Company is entitled to protect' and the Consultant has an obligation to maintain the company's Confidential Information. By weakening the return obligation, the modification creates a conflict, making the contract internally inconsistent regarding the consultant's duty to protect confidential information upon termination. The change conflicts with the proprietary rights as defined in Section 3.1 (b).
